C005.summary: P000 success: server-side SQLite residue is removed from current runtime paths and guarded.

P000.description:
Postgres is now the intended server-side persistence boundary, but the repository still contains active-looking SQLite runtime branches, startup flags, migration CLIs, admin scripts, comments, and tests. This residue makes future humans and AI likely to reintroduce queue.db, gateway.db, device.db, entangled.db, or Cortex operational.sqlite3 paths after the cutover.

Scope is server-side production and developer runtime code in this workspace. Client-local cache SQLite is out of scope unless it crosses into server persistence. Archived historical docs are lower priority unless they are referenced by current runbooks or scripts.
success_criteria: Server startup paths no longer pass or document SQLite database files for migrated services.
Queue, Gateway, Device, Entangled, and Cortex current runtime entry points no longer expose SQLite as the normal or fallback server backend after the Postgres cutover.
Migration/admin scripts that only exist to operate old SQLite server databases are removed or moved out of executable current paths.
Current tests and residue guards reflect the Postgres-only server shape instead of preserving SQLite compatibility as a goal.
Any remaining SQLite references are either client-local cache, historical archive, or explicitly justified non-server test substrate with a follow-up if not removable in this pass.
P000.title: Remove server-side SQLite code residue after Postgres cutover

P001.description: Current root startup scripts and service launch arguments still pass server SQLite database paths after the Postgres cutover, including Entangled and Cortex paths. These entry points are the highest-risk residue because they define how future deployments are copied or restarted.
success_criteria: Current startup scripts launch migrated services with Postgres backend arguments or explicit Postgres DSN files.
Current startup scripts no longer pass `entangled.db`, `operational.sqlite3`, `gateway.db`, `device.db`, `business.db`, or `queue.db` paths as server persistence.
Existing unrelated root worktree edits are preserved.
A focused residue scan over current startup/deployment paths confirms no server SQLite DB launch arguments remain.
P001.title: Remove SQLite from current startup and deployment entry points

P002.description: Queue Service still exposes SQLite backend choices, queue.db path construction, migration CLIs, and tests that preserve SQLite compatibility as current behavior. This directly conflicts with the Postgres-only server persistence target.
success_criteria: Queue Service runtime entry points use Postgres as the only server backend and no longer offer a SQLite backend selector.
Queue worker database assembly no longer constructs or logs queue.db paths.
Old Queue SQLite-to-Postgres migration CLIs/modules/tests are removed from current executable paths.
Focused Queue tests and residue scans pass for the Postgres-only boundary.
P002.title: Remove Queue Service SQLite runtime fallback

P003.description: Gateway and Device still contain SQLite backend defaults, gateway.db admin/migration scripts, and device local DB modules/comments. These paths make deleted gateway.db/device.db files look recoverable and supported.
success_criteria: Gateway startup defaults to Postgres-only auth/config storage and rejects SQLite backend selection in current server runtime.
Gateway SQLite migration/admin scripts are deleted from current executable paths or replaced with Postgres-only equivalents if still needed.
Device local SQLite modules and comments that claim active SQLite ownership are deleted or rewritten to the Entangled/EntityStore current path.
Focused Gateway/Device tests or imports pass after deletion.
P003.title: Remove Gateway and Device server SQLite utilities

P004.description: Cortex operational state and Entangled entity engine still expose SQLite as default or selectable server persistence. Migration scripts and comments also teach old SQLite operational stores as current.
success_criteria: Cortex runtime entry point defaults to Postgres operational store and does not accept SQLite as a production fallback.
Entangled runtime entry point defaults to Postgres and no longer launches from SQLite db-path in current startup examples.
Old Cortex/Entangled SQLite migration CLIs are retired from current executable paths when no longer needed.
Remaining SQLite code, if any, is limited to non-production tests or explicitly recorded as a follow-up with reason.
P004.title: Remove Cortex and Entangled server SQLite fallback paths

P005.description: After code deletion, tests and guard scripts must stop encoding SQLite compatibility as a desirable server property. The work also needs durable evidence so future cleanup does not regress.
success_criteria: Tests/guard scripts assert Postgres-only server persistence where relevant.
A residue inventory categorizes remaining SQLite references into current allowed, historical archive, or follow-up-required.
The ledger records evidence from diffs, scans, and test runs.
Touched submodules and root pointers are committed and pushed without reverting unrelated worktree changes.
P005.title: Update residue guards, tests, and ledger evidence

Wider `pytest --ignore=tests/unit/task_queue/test_payload_tool_handlers.py` ran 832 tests with 817 passing and 15 failing; the failures are existing broader-suite issues around old tests/docs/fixtures and Postgres FSM-store payload expectations, not the removed Queue SQLite runtime fallback.

acceptance_criteria: Startup scripts no longer pass server SQLite file paths for migrated services.
Current server runtime entry points reject or omit SQLite backend selection for migrated services.
SQLite migration/admin utilities for old server DB files are deleted from current executable paths.
Tests no longer encode SQLite fallback as desired server behavior.
Remaining SQLite references are enumerated and justified by category.
assumptions: Server-side production persistence is Postgres-only after the migration.
Client-local SQLite caches are out of scope for this cleanup pass.
Historical docs archives can remain if they are not imported, executed, or referenced by current runbooks.
T000.classification_reason: Touches multiple service boundaries and requires staged deletion plus tests.
T000.problem_definition: Postgres cutover is complete for server persistence, but current code still contains SQLite runtime switches, server database file arguments, migration/admin CLIs, and tests that assert SQLite compatibility. These paths are misleading because they look executable after the cutover.
T000.proposed_solution: Split the cleanup by active service boundary. First remove production startup and runtime selector residue. Then retire old migration/admin scripts and update tests/residue guards so Postgres-only server persistence is the current path. Keep only references that are clearly client-local cache or historical archive, and label any remaining low-level SQLite substrate as a separately closed follow-up if it cannot be deleted without replacing a still-active abstraction.
risks: Some low-level SQLite adapters may still be used as generic test stores and require a separate Postgres-backed replacement rather than blind deletion.
Existing unrelated root worktree changes must be preserved while editing shared scripts.
Production runtime may rely on env/secret files not present locally, so local verification may be limited to import/unit/residue checks unless remote deploy smoke is performed.
T000.title: Delete migrated server SQLite code paths
T000.verification_plan:
- Run targeted ripgrep residue scans across current service directories.
- Run focused unit tests for changed service boundaries.
- Run ledger success check with evidence from diffs, scans, and tests.
- Commit and push each touched submodule plus the root pointer/ledger changes.

acceptance_criteria: `scripts/start.sh` no longer contains `entangled.db` or `operational.sqlite3` server launch arguments.
Entangled launches with `--db-backend postgres --postgres-dsn-file`.
Gateway launches with `--db-backend postgres --postgres-dsn-file`.
Cortex launches with `--operational-backend postgres --operational-postgres-dsn-file`.
A focused scan over current startup/deploy files reports no old server SQLite launch args.
assumptions: `/opt/novaic/secrets/*-postgres-dsn` is the intended family of secret files used by the Docker/API machine layout.
T001.classification_reason: Single startup script plus one current runbook line; bounded and verifiable by shell syntax plus residue scan.
T001.problem_definition: The root Linux/cloud startup script still passes server SQLite database files for Entangled and Cortex and does not pass Postgres DSN files for those services. Gateway also starts without Postgres backend flags.
T001.proposed_solution: Patch `scripts/start.sh` so migrated server services start with explicit Postgres backend flags and DSN files under `/opt/novaic/secrets`. Add shell variables for the current Postgres DSN file paths, remove server SQLite file arguments, and preserve the existing unrelated script edits. Update any current deploy runbook line that still describes a SQLite gateway.db operation when it is part of current docs.
risks: The exact production DSN filenames must match deployed secret paths; choose stable service-specific filenames under `/opt/novaic/secrets` and align later deploy if production differs.
T001.title: Make current startup Postgres-only
T001.verification_plan:
- Run `bash -n scripts/start.sh`.
- Run `rg` against `scripts/start.sh` and current deploy docs for `.db`/SQLite launch residue.
- Inspect `git diff -- scripts/start.sh docs/runbooks/deploy.md` to ensure the change is scoped.

acceptance_criteria: `main_novaic.py queue-service` no longer accepts `--db-backend sqlite` or sets `NOVAIC_QUEUE_DB_BACKEND`.
`queue_service/main.py` no longer constructs `queue.db` or reports SQLite readiness.
`task_queue/workers/assembly_factories.py` no longer reads `NOVAIC_QUEUE_DB_BACKEND` or constructs `queue.db`.
Old Queue SQLite migration CLI/modules/tests are deleted from current paths.
Focused Queue Postgres tests and residue scans pass.
assumptions: Queue server production and integration-test environments have `/opt/novaic/postgres/secrets/novaic_queue_dsn` or an equivalent DSN file.
T002.classification_reason: One service boundary with concrete runtime/migration deletion and focused tests; deeper low-level test substrate can be judged as a gap if it remains.
T002.problem_definition: Queue Service still carries active-looking SQLite runtime fallback: CLI `--db-backend sqlite`, env backend switches, queue.db construction, SQLite migration CLIs, and SQLite lock boundary helpers. These make the old queue.db path look supported after the Postgres cutover.
T002.proposed_solution: Remove the Queue runtime backend switch and make Postgres the only server runtime database. Delete old SQLite-to-Postgres migration modules/tests from current executable paths. Replace worker DB assembly with explicit Postgres DSN inputs, and update the supervised worker roster/startup commands for outbox workers that open the Queue DB directly.
risks: Many older tests use `common.db.Database(tmp_path / queue.sqlite3)` as a cheap unit-test substrate; removing all of that in one step may require a larger Postgres test fixture refactor.
Some repository SQL helpers still generate SQLite dialect branches for old tests; this ticket focuses on server runtime fallback, not every low-level historical dialect branch.
T002.title: Make Queue Service Postgres-only at runtime
T002.verification_plan:
- Run targeted Queue runtime tests after updating expectations.
- Run `python -m py_compile` on changed Queue modules.
- Run `rg` for `NOVAIC_QUEUE_DB_BACKEND`, `queue.db`, `migrate_sqlite_to_postgres`, and `sqlite_boundary` in Queue runtime paths.

acceptance_criteria: Gateway current runtime no longer defaults to or accepts `--db-backend sqlite`.
Gateway DB access initializes `PostgresGatewayDatabase` only and requires a Postgres DSN file.
Current executable scripts that mutate or migrate `gateway.db` are deleted from root/Gateway paths.
Device local SQLite DB module is deleted if unused, and Device comments no longer claim local SQLite storage ownership.
Focused Gateway/Device imports/tests and residue scans pass.
assumptions: `/opt/novaic/postgres/secrets/novaic_gateway_dsn` is the production Gateway auth/config DSN file.
T003.classification_reason: Two service utility surfaces with clear files to delete/update and focused compile/residue checks.
T003.problem_definition: Gateway still defaults to SQLite auth/config storage and ships scripts that directly mutate `gateway.db`. Device still has an executable local SQLite DB module and comments that describe SQLite as the current storage owner. These contradict the Postgres/Entangled server-state target and make deleted DB files look supported.
T003.proposed_solution: Make Gateway runtime Postgres-only by removing SQLite backend selection and db-file configuration from the current entry point/access layer. Delete executable SQLite migration/admin scripts in Gateway/current root scripts. Remove the Device local SQLite DB module if unused, and rewrite misleading Device comments to EntityStore/Entangled ownership.
risks: Some tests may still use SQLite schema validators or client-side cache references; those should be categorized rather than silently kept as server runtime paths.
T003.title: Remove Gateway and Device SQLite current-path utilities
T003.verification_plan:
- Run py-compile for changed Gateway/Device modules.
- Run focused Gateway Postgres tests and Device import checks.
- Run residue scans for `gateway.db`, `device.db`, `sqlite3`, and `--db-backend` over current Gateway/Device runtime paths.

acceptance_criteria: Cortex startup CLI no longer accepts or requires an operational SQLite path.
Entangled startup CLI no longer advertises SQLite db-path startup as normal server mode.
Old Cortex/Entangled SQLite migration CLIs are deleted from current executable paths.
Root `scripts/start.sh` matches the new Postgres-only CLI surface.
Focused py-compile/residue scans pass, and any remaining SQLite store substrate is explicitly categorized.
assumptions: Production Cortex and Entangled should be started only against `/opt/novaic/postgres/secrets/novaic_cortex_dsn` and `/opt/novaic/postgres/secrets/novaic_entangled_dsn`.
T004.classification_reason: Bounded to startup CLI fallback and migration-script deletion; deeper store refactor remains explicitly categorized.
T004.problem_definition: Cortex and Entangled still expose SQLite fallback paths through service CLIs, default configs, and old migration scripts. After the Postgres cutover these are misleading current paths even when production startup already aims at Postgres.
T004.proposed_solution: Make Cortex and Entangled server entry points Postgres-only where practical, delete old SQLite migration CLIs from current paths, and update root startup to match. Preserve only lower-level store code that cannot be safely deleted in this pass without replacing active abstractions, and record it explicitly as residue for the guard/follow-up phase.
risks: Cortex's Postgres operational store currently subclasses the SQLite store implementation, so deleting all SQLite-named substrate requires a larger refactor.
Entangled SQL entity definitions may still support SQLite dialect generation for tests or historical clients.
T004.title: Remove Cortex and Entangled SQLite server runtime fallbacks
T004.verification_plan:
- Run py-compile on changed Cortex/Entangled modules.
